response: solution The erroneous code likely fails by not handling duplicate elements properly when the most frequent number exceeds half the array length, causing adjacent equal values. solution A correct approach involves finding the median, then partitioning the array into smaller and larger elements while interleaving them to avoid adjacent duplicates. solution To achieve O(n) time without full sorting, use a selection algorithm to find the median, then perform a virtual index mapping to place elements in odd and even positions correctly. solution This method works for both positive and negative integers since it relies only on relative comparisons and partitioning logic. solution The final implementation ensures that no two adjacent elements are equal whenever the input allows a valid wiggle sort configuration.
